options.content:
## Argo Workflows CI Observability

Real-time and historical view of the **Argo Workflows controller** (`workflow-controller`) running container-native CI pipelines in this cluster. Tracks every Workflow from submission through completion, with its pods scheduled on the **Spot `ci-spot`** capacity.

**Five signals this dashboard tracks**
- 🟢 **Health** — are workflows succeeding? → *Workflow Success Rate*, *Workflows by Phase*, *Outcome Distribution*
- ⚡ **Speed** — how long do workflows take? → *Workflow Duration* (mean), *Mean Workflow Duration*
- 📦 **Capacity** — is the controller keeping up? → *Running Workflows*, *Workflow Pods*
- 📊 **Velocity** — how fast is CI delivering? → *CI Throughput*, *Total Workflows*
- 📋 **Logs & Traces** — what happened in a specific run? → *Workflow Pod Logs*, *Traces*, *Log Error Rate*, *Log Volume by Level*

> **Scope:** use **Status**, **Namespace**, and **Log Namespace** filters to narrow every panel · Metrics come from the `workflow-controller` Prometheus endpoint (`argo_workflows_*`); pod-level signals from kube-state-metrics. Works in **all four** observability backends.

**Step 1 · Read the KPI cards (top row)**
- **Running Workflows** — workflows executing *right now* (`argo_workflows_count{status="Running"}`)
- **Workflows (range)** — total completions in the window
- **Success Rate** — 🟢 ≥ 99 % · 🟡 ≥ 80 % · 🔴 below 80 %
- **Workflow Pods** — `argo_workflows_pods_count` currently active

**Step 2 · Read the trend charts**
*Workflows by Phase* shows Running/Succeeded/Failed/Error/Pending over time. A rising `Failed`/`Error` stack means a step is erroring.

**Step 3 · Narrow with the Log level filter**
Set **Log level → error** so *Workflow Pod Logs* shows only failure events from the workflow pods.

**Step 4 · Drill into a Trace**
Click any **Trace ID** in *Workflow Traces* to open the span waterfall for that run.

**Duration regression**
If *Mean Workflow Duration* climbs while throughput is flat → a step got slower or the cluster is resource-constrained; open *Traces* for the longest span and check node CPU/memory.

**Workflows stuck Pending**
*Workflow Pods* not rising while *Running Workflows* climbs → pods can't schedule (Spot node cold-start / preemption / quota). Cross-check the **Node Auto-Provisioning** board.

**Queue build-up**
*Running Workflows* climbing without matching completions → backlog. Check the controller: `kubectl top pod -n argo` and the `argo_workflows_workers_busy` / queue-depth metrics.

**Key metric reference**

| Metric | Type | What it tracks |
|---|---|---|
| `argo_workflows_count{status}` | Gauge | Workflows by phase (Running/Succeeded/Failed/Error/Pending) |
| `argo_workflows_pods_count` | Gauge | Active workflow pods |
| `argo_workflows_operation_duration_seconds` | Histogram | Controller operation (reconcile) duration |
| `argo_workflows_workers_busy` | Gauge | Busy controller workers (queue pressure) |
| `argo_workflows_queue_depth_count` | Gauge | Workqueue depth |

panels.description: Workflows currently in Failed/Error status. Yellow ≥1, Red ≥5.
panels.title: Failed Workflows

panels.description: Active workflow pods (argo_workflows_pods_count).
panels.title: Workflow Pods

panels.description: Workflow completions per hour over the selected range.
panels.title: CI Throughput (runs/h)

panels.description: Mean controller operation duration as a proxy for workflow processing time. Yellow ≥5 min, Red ≥15 min.
panels.title: Mean Workflow Duration

panels.description: Rolling share of workflows in Failed/Error status. Yellow >5%, Red >20%.
panels.title: Workflow Failure Rate

panels.description: Running workflows and active workflow pods — indicates controller/executor utilization.
panels.title: Concurrent Workflows & Pods (Executor Utilization)

panels.description: Donut breakdown of all workflow phases in the current snapshot.
panels.title: Workflow Outcome Distribution

panels.description: Workflow counts by phase and namespace.
panels.title: Workflows by Phase & Namespace
Argo Workflows CI — workflows & pods (controller metrics)

panels.description: Workflows currently executing (argo_workflows_count{status="Running"}).
panels.title: Running Workflows

panels.description: Workflow pods currently active (argo_workflows_pods_count).
panels.title: Active Workflow Pods

panels.description: Share of workflows Succeeded vs all terminal workflows in snapshot.
panels.title: Workflow Success Rate

panels.description: Total workflows in all phases right now (sum of argo_workflows_count).
panels.title: Total Workflows

panels.description: Workflow count by phase over time (argo_workflows_count{status}).
panels.title: Workflows by Phase

panels.description: workflow-controller operation (reconcile) duration percentiles (argo_workflows_operation_duration_seconds).
panels.title: Controller Operation Duration (p50 / p95 / mean)

panels.description: Argo workflow pods by phase over time (kube-state-metrics, ns argo). Complements the controller's argo_workflows_pods_count gauge.
panels.title: Workflow Pods by Phase

panels.description: workflow-controller workqueue depth & busy workers — rising depth means the controller can't keep up.
panels.title: Controller Queue Pressure

panels.description: Logs from the Argo Workflows execution namespaces (the workflow pods).
panels.title: Workflow Pod Logs

panels.description: Distributed traces emitted by the workflow steps (OTLP to the collector). Drill into a single workflow's spans.
panels.title: Workflow Traces

panels.description: Rate of error-level log lines. Spikes indicate runner/workflow pod failures.
panels.title: Log Error Rate

panels.description: Log volume broken down by severity level. Stacked view highlights relative noise across info/warn/error.
panels.title: Log Volume by Level

list.description: Filter log panels by severity (detected_level). All = everything incl. plain-text (unknown).
list.label: Log level
list.name: level

title: CI-CD / Argo Workflows CI Observability
description: Argo Workflows container-native CI. Metrics from the workflow-controller Prometheus endpoint (argo_workflows_count{status}, argo_workflows_pods_count, argo_workflows_operation_duration_seconds), complemented by kube-state-metrics pod phases — the lean slice 03-observability.sh keeps in every observability mode, so this board works across all four backends.
